spec_match.rationale: Creates a real `AudioContext`, plays a 220 Hz oscillator, and walks the user through `suspended → running → interrupted → running`. That is the exact new state the spec adds (`AudioContextState.interrupted`).

interactive.rationale: Working play/pause/resume buttons with 5 listeners drive a live state event log; the feature-detect probe at the top reads `'interrupted' in AudioContextState`-style.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises `Press play to start a 220 Hz sine, then trigger a real or simulated interruption` and the page is exactly that, with a working sine-wave path.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers running→interrupted→running, but never demonstrates the terminal `closed` state, attempting to resume from `closed` (rejects), or what happens if interrupted fires while the context is already suspended by the user.

references.rationale: `see also` cites https://chromestatus.com/feature/5172068166139904 and the Web Audio API spec — both required references are present.

design_system.rationale: Inline styles use CSS variables only, no raw hex detected, no button-color/background mismatch.

openQuestions.title: Show the terminal `closed` state path
openQuestions.detail: The state machine has a terminal `closed` node that none of the buttons reach. Add a `close` button that calls `audioContext.close()` and shows that subsequent calls to `resume()`/`suspend()` reject with InvalidStateError — that is the cliff-edge case authors usually miss.

openQuestions.title: Cover interrupted-while-suspended
openQuestions.detail: If the user has already manually suspended the context and the OS then interrupts audio focus, the spec needs to define a stable outcome. Add a flow that explicitly suspends, then simulates an interruption, and surfaces the resulting state — readers should not have to wonder which state wins.

openQuestions.title: Surface the audio-session policy hooks
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary mentions `exclusive audio access (VoIP) or when a laptop lid is closed` as triggering interruptions. Pair the demo with a short doc strip explaining how `navigator.audioSession.type` (related spec) influences when the UA will fire interrupted, so the page sits in context against the broader audio-session work.

summary: Clean canonical demo of the new `interrupted` state — real oscillator, working buttons, correct references. Worth extending with the `closed` terminal-state path and the interrupted-while-suspended edge so the state machine teaching is complete.
